fact: July 19 is exactly 200 days into the year! That means there are only 165 days left until New Year's Eve.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this time of year in North America! These bug musicians can make sounds as loud as a motorcycle revving up.
July 19 falls in the middle of Cancer season in the zodiac - people born on this day are supposedly extra moody like crabs!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 19 is deep winter, which means penguins are huddling together for warmth while we're sweating!
Sunflowers are usually at their most gigantic around this date in northern climates! Some can grow taller than a basketball player and their heads can weigh as much as a bowling ball.
The sun rises before 6 AM and sets after 8 PM in most of North America on July 19, giving kids extra daylight for catching fireflies!
Bears are getting super chubby right now in North America! July is prime berry season, so bears stuff themselves with up to 20,000 calories per day - that's like eating 80 hamburgers!
This date is roughly one month after the summer solstice, which means we're starting to lose about 2 minutes of daylight each day (but it's too small to notice).
Mosquitoes are typically at their most annoying peak around July 19 in temperate regions because the hot, humid weather makes them super active and hungry for blood!
Ocean water temperatures often hit their warmest in many places around this time, making it perfect for swimming but also creating more powerful thunderstorms!
